description: 55M, HTN, CKD 3a (eGFR 52), LDL 145, ASCVD risk 8.5%. ACC/AHA R4 (COR I) and KDIGO statin-for-CKD (1A) both recommend statin. USPSTF Grade C also matched but is preempted by ACC/AHA (P4, ADR 0018). CKD context favors moderate intensity.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A for primary prevention (ASCVD risk ≥7.5%, LDL 70-189); KDIGO 1A for CKD statin therapy. USPSTF Grade C also matched but is preempted by ACC/AHA (ADR 0018). CKD G3a (eGFR 52) context favors moderate intensity. CKD is a cardiovascular risk enhancer that strengthens the case for initiation.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR for CKD staging and progression tracking. CKD G3a (eGFR 52) with normal albuminuria (ACR 18) indicates moderate risk. Monitor at least annually.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 140/86 mmHg is above target. HTN is both a CVD risk factor and an accelerator of CKD progression. Target BP <130/80 per KDIGO for CKD patients.
